summary:
The peak alpha-fetoprotein levels are found in fetal serum due to its initial synthesis there; these concentrations then decrease as the fetus develops further into infancy without significant ongoing hepatic or other tissue expression that could sustain such elevated values postnatally. Thus,

Maximum alpha-fetoprotein levels are observed in fetal serum at birth but decline rapidly thereafter unless specific conditions like hepatoblastoma persistently elevate them beyond normal neonatal ranges.
